generatedSentence: In the `vast`, `dusky` `byways` of a forgotten realm, one could `imagine` the `lengthy` `downsides` of their `destiny`. `Wally`, an `old`, `husky` `defender` of forgotten lore, meticulously organized his `distaff` collection of `clay` tablets, each inscribed with a `powdery` `alphabet`. His `persnickety` `nature` meant every `feature` of his `resting` `waypoint` near the `firepit` was `docile`, yet held `taciturn` wisdom under a perpetual `mist`. This `phantasy` of a serene life was his constant companion. However, a `scandalous` `machination` unfolded on the `whiteboard` of the city's corrupt `editors`. A `demanding` `mammon`-fueled `dealer`, known for his `erotic` `passion` for power, had hired `gaffers` and `vandals` to `decorate` the `highway` with `filthy` `pimples` of propaganda, using `astrology` to predict a `vague` `earthquake`. The `obnoxious` `goalkeeper` of the city's `jail`, a `husky` `bulldog` of a man, `flashed` a `vee` sign as a `wayward` `parade` of `intoxicated` citizens marched towards the `palace`, their `latency` for rebellion simmering. The `kickoff` for chaos began as `keypads` on the `flagship` `lobby` were `whacks` with `bolts`, initiating a system `break`. `Archer`-bots, clad in `waders` for the `wet` `bayside` slums, began a `screeching` `retcon` of reality, their `gender` programming `bleaches` all individuality. A `gamekeeper` `nanny`, holding a `raspberry` `barrel` of `nutrient` paste, watched `paparazzi` swarm, snapping `offhand` photos of `nymphs` dancing to a `funky` `negroni`-fueled `bands`. The `gateman` was `intoxicated` by the `evangelical` fervor, oblivious to the `censorship` `glue`ing shut the eyes of the populace. Amidst the `hokey` chaos, `Wally` experienced a sudden `realisation`: he needed to `comb` through the city's `downsides` to `restore` order. With newfound `immunity`, he began to play `kickball` with a lone `goat` he’d rescued, distracting the `babysitter` bots. This simple act of `restoring` hope, like a `basket` of `kebabs` offered to the downtrodden, became his `retirement` from `old` ways. `Wally`, now an `usher` of change, fought the `cluck` of despair, `ball` in hand, using his `virtuoso` spirit to reclaim `destiny` from the `demanding` darkness, one `break` at a time.
panelSentences: In the `vast`, `dusky` `byways` of a forgotten realm, one could `imagine` the `lengthy` `downsides` of their `destiny`. `Wally`, an `old`, `husky` `defender` of forgotten lore, meticulously organized his `distaff` collection of `clay` tablets, each inscribed with a `powdery` `alphabet`. His `persnickety` `nature` meant every `feature` of his `resting` `waypoint` near the `firepit` was `docile`, yet held `taciturn` wisdom under a perpetual `mist`. This `phantasy` of a serene life was his constant companion.

However, a `scandalous` `machination` unfolded on the `whiteboard` of the city's corrupt `editors`. A `demanding` `mammon`-fueled `dealer`, known for his `erotic` `passion` for power, had hired `gaffers` and `vandals` to `decorate` the `highway` with `filthy` `pimples` of propaganda, using `astrology` to predict a `vague` `earthquake`. The `obnoxious` `goalkeeper` of the city's `jail`, a `husky` `bulldog` of a man, `flashed` a `vee` sign as a `wayward` `parade` of `intoxicated` citizens marched towards the `palace`, their `latency` for rebellion simmering.

The `kickoff` for chaos began as `keypads` on the `flagship` `lobby` were `whacks` with `bolts`, initiating a system `break`. `Archer`-bots, clad in `waders` for the `wet` `bayside` slums, began a `screeching` `retcon` of reality, their `gender` programming `bleaches` all individuality. A `gamekeeper` `nanny`, holding a `raspberry` `barrel` of `nutrient` paste, watched `paparazzi` swarm, snapping `offhand` photos of `nymphs` dancing to a `funky` `negroni`-fueled `bands`. The `gateman` was `intoxicated` by the `evangelical` fervor, oblivious to the `censorship` `glue`ing shut the eyes of the populace.

Amidst the `hokey` chaos, `Wally` experienced a sudden `realisation`: he needed to `comb` through the city's `downsides` to `restore` order. With newfound `immunity`, he began to play `kickball` with a lone `goat` he’d rescued, distracting the `babysitter` bots. This simple act of `restoring` hope, like a `basket` of `kebabs` offered to the downtrodden, became his `retirement` from `old` ways. `Wally`, now an `usher` of change, fought the `cluck` of despair, `ball` in hand, using his `virtuoso` spirit to reclaim `destiny` from the `demanding` darkness, one `break` at a time.
rawPanelSentences: In the `vast`, `dusky` `byways` of a forgotten realm, one could `imagine` the `lengthy` `downsides` of their `destiny`. `Wally`, an `old`, `husky` `defender` of forgotten lore, meticulously organized his `distaff` collection of `clay` tablets, each inscribed with a `powdery` `alphabet`. His `persnickety` `nature` meant every `feature` of his `resting` `waypoint` near the `firepit` was `docile`, yet held `taciturn` wisdom under a perpetual `mist`. This `phantasy` of a serene life was his constant companion.

However, a `scandalous` `machination` unfolded on the `whiteboard` of the city's corrupt `editors`. A `demanding` `mammon`-fueled `dealer`, known for his `erotic` `passion` for power, had hired `gaffers` and `vandals` to `decorate` the `highway` with `filthy` `pimples` of propaganda, using `astrology` to predict a `vague` `earthquake`. The `obnoxious` `goalkeeper` of the city's `jail`, a `husky` `bulldog` of a man, `flashed` a `vee` sign as a `wayward` `parade` of `intoxicated` citizens marched towards the `palace`, their `latency` for rebellion simmering.

The `kickoff` for chaos began as `keypads` on the `flagship` `lobby` were `whacks` with `bolts`, initiating a system `break`. `Archer`-bots, clad in `waders` for the `wet` `bayside` slums, began a `screeching` `retcon` of reality, their `gender` programming `bleaches` all individuality. A `gamekeeper` `nanny`, holding a `raspberry` `barrel` of `nutrient` paste, watched `paparazzi` swarm, snapping `offhand` photos of `nymphs` dancing to a `funky` `negroni`-fueled `bands`. The `gateman` was `intoxicated` by the `evangelical` fervor, oblivious to the `censorship` `glue`ing shut the eyes of the populace.

Amidst the `hokey` chaos, `Wally` experienced a sudden `realisation`: he needed to `comb` through the city's `downsides` to `restore` order. With newfound `immunity`, he began to play `kickball` with a lone `goat` he’d rescued, distracting the `babysitter` bots. This simple act of `restoring` hope, like a `basket` of `kebabs` offered to the downtrodden, became his `retirement` from `old` ways. `Wally`, now an `usher` of change, fought the `cluck` of despair, `ball` in hand, using his `virtuoso` spirit to reclaim `destiny` from the `demanding` darkness, one `break` at a time.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone, elderly man with a long, flowing white beard, wearing a patchwork cloak adorned with ancient symbols and carrying a gnarled staff topped with a glowing crystal. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Wally, seated cross-legged by a glowing firepit, meticulously examining clay tablets, with a serene, focused expression. Camera Angle: Medium shot, slightly low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A distorted, chaotic parade of shadowy figures with glowing eyes marching past a neon-lit billboard displaying a corrupt leader, rain slicking the highway. Camera Angle: Wide shot, elevated perspective. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Archer-bots with glowing red eyes clashing with holographic, spectral figures near a futuristic bay, energy bolts arcing. Camera Angle: Dynamic low angle, focus on action. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Wally, now standing tall, kicking a glowing energy ball towards a monstrous, shadowy figure, a sense of hope radiating from him as the chaotic environment begins to recede into light. Camera Angle: Heroic full shot, dramatic lighting.

definitions.definition: A road that is not frequently travelled
(by extension) an unpopular or arcane field of study

A breed of dog developed in England by the crossing of the bullbaiting dog and the Pug to produce a ladies companion dog. Having a very smooth coat, a flattened face, wrinkly cheeks, powerful front legs and smaller hind legs.
British bulldog
A stubborn person.
A refractory material used as a furnace lining, obtained by calcining the cinder or slag from the puddling furnace of a rolling mill.
(Oxford University slang) One of the proctors' officers.
(pro wrestling) Any move in which the wrestler grabs an opponent's head and jumps forward, so that the wrestler lands, often in a sitting position, and drives the opponent's face into the mat.
A bulldog edition.
To chase (a steer) on horseback and wrestle it to the ground by twisting its horns (as a rodeo performance).
A short fanfic or prompt placing a reader insert in a novel scenario with a character or celebrity.
To form a mental image of something; to envision or create something in one's mind.
definitions.example: Try to imagine a pink elephant.

definitions.definition: To believe in something created by one's own mind.
definitions.example: She imagined that the man wanted to kill her.

definitions.definition: To assume
definitions.example: I imagine that he will need to rest after such a long flight.

definitions.definition: To conjecture or guess
definitions.example: I cannot even imagine what you are up to!

definitions.definition: To use one's imagination
definitions.example: Imagine that we were siblings.

definitions.definition: To contrive in purpose; to scheme; to devise.

A device to which a bundle of natural fibres (often wool, flax, or cotton) are attached for temporary storage, before being drawn off gradually to spin thread. A traditional distaff is a staff with flax fibres tied loosely to it (as indicated by the etymology of the word), but modern distaffs are often made of cords weighted with beads, and attached to the wrist.
The part of a spinning wheel from which fibre is drawn to be spun.
Anything traditionally done by or considered of importance to women only.
A race for female horses only.
A woman, or women considered as a group.
Of, relating to, or characteristic of women.
Of the maternal side of a family.
An amorous composition or poem.
Relating to or tending to arouse sexual desire or excitement.
definitions.example: Foreplay can get your erotic juices flowing.

definitions.definition: To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
definitions.example: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

definitions.definition: To fix or determine the value of; assign a value to, as of jewelry or art work.
To regard highly; think much of; place importance upon.
definitions.example: Gold was valued highly among the Romans.

definitions.definition: To hold dear.
definitions.example: I value these old photographs.

definitions.definition: A fool
(London and Essex) a large pickled gherkin or cucumber
(Of eyes) unusually pale; misaligned, sideways-looking, affected by strabismus.
A vast space.
Very large or wide (literally or figuratively).
definitions.example: The Sahara desert is vast.

definitions.definition: Very great in size, amount, degree, intensity, or especially extent.
Waste; desert; desolate; lonely.
Fussy; paying undue attention to minor details; fastidious.
Requiring attention to minor details.
A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.
Confinement in a jail.
The condition created by the requirement that a horse claimed in a claiming race not be run at another track for some period of time (usually 30 days).
In dodgeball and related games, the area where players who have been struck by the ball are confined.
(FreeBSD) A kind of sandbox for running a guest operating system instance.
To imprison.
A toothed implement for grooming the hair or (formerly) for keeping it in place.
A machine used in separating choice cotton fibers from worsted cloth fibers.
A fleshy growth on the top of the head of some birds and reptiles; crest.
A structure of hexagon cells made by bees for storing honey; honeycomb.
An old English measure of corn equal to the half quarter.
The top part of a gun’s stock.
The toothed plate at the top and bottom of an escalator that prevents objects getting trapped between the moving stairs and fixed landings.
The main body of a harmonica containing the air chambers and to which the reed plates are attached.
A former, commonly cone-shaped, used in hat manufacturing for hardening soft fibre.
A toothed tool used for chasing screws on work in a lathe; a chaser.
The notched scale of a wire micrometer.
The collector of an electrical machine, usually resembling a comb.
One of a pair of peculiar organs on the base of the abdomen in scorpions.
The curling crest of a wave; a comber.
A toothed plate used for creating wells in agar gels for electrophoresis.
A toothed wooden pick used to push the weft thread tightly against the previous pass of thread to create a tight weave.

A connected and reduced curve with irreducible components consisting of a smooth subcurve (called the handle) and one or more additional irreducible components (called teeth) that each intersect the handle in a single point that is unequal to the unique point of intersection for any of the other teeth.
(especially of hair or fur) To groom with a toothed implement; chiefly with a comb.
definitions.example: I need to comb my hair before we leave the house

definitions.definition: To separate choice cotton fibers from worsted cloth fibers.
To search thoroughly as if raking over an area with a comb.
definitions.example: Police combed the field for evidence after the assault

definitions.definition: To roll over, as the top or crest of a wave; to break with a white foam, as waves.
A chief lighting technician for a motion-picture or television production.
A glassblower.
An old man.
A foreman.
A sailor.
(in Maritime regions) The baby in the house.
Having length; long and overextended, especially in time rather than dimension.
definitions.example: She died last night after a lengthy illness.

definitions.definition: Speaking or writing at length; long-winded.
A disadvantageous aspect of something that is normally advantageous.
definitions.example: The downside of obtaining a higher rank is that far more work is expected.

definitions.definition: A downward tendency, especially in the price of shares etc.
That to which any person or thing is destined; a predetermined state; a condition predestined by the Divine or by human will
That which is inevitable in the fullness of time.
The fixed order of things; invincible necessity; an irresistible power or agency conceived of as determining the future, whether in general or of an individual.
Someone who defends people or property
One of the players whose primary task is to prevent the opposition from scoring
A fighter who seeks to repel an attack
A lawyer who represents defendants, especially a public defender; a defense attorney (US) or defence counsel (UK)
A defendant in a civil action
The sound of a heavy strike.
The strike itself.
The stroke itself, regardless of its successful impact.
An attempt, a chance, a turn, a go, originally an attempt to beat someone or something.
definitions.example: 40 bucks a whack.

definitions.definition: (originally Britain cant) A share, a portion, especially a full share or large portion.
A whack-up: a division of an amount into separate whacks, a divvying up.
A deal, an agreement.
definitions.example: It's a whack!

definitions.definition: The backslash, ⟨ \ ⟩.
To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
To make very dirty; to cover in filth.
Covered with filth; very dirty.
Obscene or offensive.
Very unpleasant or disagreeable.
A pit dug in the ground, or constructed from stones, in which a fire is made, principally for cooking.
A wandering; a vagary.
An indefinite expanse.
To wander; to roam; to stray.
To become vague or act in a vague manner.
Not clearly expressed; stated in indefinite terms.
Not having a precise meaning.
definitions.example: a vague term of abuse

definitions.definition: Not clearly defined, grasped, or understood; indistinct; slight.
definitions.example: I haven’t the vaguest idea.

definitions.definition: Not clearly felt or sensed; somewhat subconscious.
definitions.example: a vague longing

definitions.definition: Not thinking or expressing one’s thoughts clearly or precisely.
Lacking expression; vacant.
Not sharply outlined; hazy.
Wandering; vagrant; vagabond.
An inflamed (raised and colored) spot on the surface of the skin that is usually painful and fills with pus.
definitions.example: I had to pop that embarrassing pimple, it was huge and red and on the tip of my nose.

definitions.definition: An annoying person.
definitions.example: He's such a pimple! I wish he'd stop being so irritating!

definitions.definition: Scotch (whisky)
To develop pimples
The natural world; that which consists of all things unaffected by or predating human technology, production, and design. (Compare ecosystem.)
definitions.example: Nature never lies (i.e. tells untruths).

definitions.definition: The innate characteristics of a thing. What something will tend by its own constitution, to be or do. Distinct from what might be expected or intended.
The summary of everything that has to do with biological, chemical and physical states and events in the physical universe.
Conformity to that which is natural, as distinguished from that which is artificial, or forced, or remote from actual experience.
Kind, sort; character; quality.
Physical constitution or existence; the vital powers; the natural life.
Natural affection or reverence.
To endow with natural qualities.
(Greek, Roman) Any female nature spirit associated with water, forests, grotto, wind, etc.
A young girl, especially one who is attractive, beautiful or graceful.
The larva of certain insects.
Any of various butterflies of the family Nymphalidae.
A source of nourishment, such as food, that can be metabolized by an organism to give energy and build tissue.
Providing nourishment.
Water or other liquid finely suspended in air.
definitions.example: It was difficult to see through the morning mist.

definitions.definition: A layer of fine droplets or particles.
definitions.example: There was an oily mist on the lens.

definitions.definition: Anything that dims, darkens, or hinders vision.
To form mist.
definitions.example: It's misting this morning.

definitions.definition: To spray fine droplets on, particularly of water.
definitions.example: I mist my tropical plants every morning.

definitions.definition: To cover with a mist.
definitions.example: The lens was misted.

definitions.definition: (of the eyes) To be covered by tears.
definitions.example: My eyes misted when I remembered what had happened.

definitions.definition: An instance of breaking something into two or more pieces.
definitions.example: The femur has a clean break and so should heal easily.

definitions.definition: A physical space that opens up in something or between two things.
definitions.example: He waited minutes for a break in the traffic to cross the highway.

definitions.definition: A rest or pause, usually from work.
definitions.example: Let’s take a five-minute break.

definitions.definition: A time for students to talk or play.
A short holiday.
definitions.example: a weekend break on the Isle of Wight

definitions.definition: A temporary split with a romantic partner.
definitions.example: I think we need a break.

definitions.definition: An interval or intermission between two parts of a performance, for example a theatre show, broadcast, or sports game.
A significant change in circumstance, attitude, perception, or focus of attention.
definitions.example: big break

definitions.definition: The beginning (of the morning).
definitions.example: at the break of day

definitions.definition: An act of escaping.
definitions.example: It was a clean break.

definitions.definition: The separation between lines, paragraphs or pages of a written text.
A keystroke or other signal that causes a program to terminate or suspend execution.
A point or condition in a program at which operation may be suspended during debugging so that the state of the program at that point can be investigated. A breakpoint.
A change, particularly the end of a spell of persistent good or bad weather.
A large four-wheeled carriage, having a straight body and calash top, with the driver's seat in front and the footman's behind.
(equitation) A sharp bit or snaffle.
A short section of music, often between verses, in which some performers stop while others continue.
definitions.example: The fiddle break was amazing; it was a pity the singer came back in on the wrong note.

definitions.definition: The point in the musical scale at which a woodwind instrument is designed to overblow, that is, to move from its lower to its upper register.
definitions.example: Crossing the break smoothly is one of the first lessons the young clarinettist needs to master.

definitions.definition: Usu. plural An area along a river that features steep banks, bluffs, or gorges (e.g., Upper Missouri River Breaks National Monument, US).
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.
definitions.example: to break flax

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.
definitions.example: to break into a run or gallop

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
Wrong, immoral, causing a scandal
Malicious, defamatory.
Outrageous; exceeding reasonable limits.

A (usually) metal fastener consisting of a cylindrical body that is threaded, with a larger head on one end. It can be inserted into an unthreaded hole up to the head, with a nut then threaded on the other end; a heavy machine screw.
A sliding pin or bar in a lock or latch mechanism.
A bar of wood or metal dropped in horizontal hooks on a door and adjoining wall or between the two sides of a double door, to prevent the door(s) from being forced open.
A sliding mechanism to chamber and unchamber a cartridge in a firearm.
A small personal-armour-piercing missile for short-range use, or (in common usage though deprecated by experts) a short arrow, intended to be shot from a crossbow or a catapult.
A lightning spark, i.e., a lightning bolt.
A sudden event, action or emotion.
definitions.example: The problem's solution struck him like a bolt from the blue.

definitions.definition: A large roll of fabric or similar material, as a bolt of cloth.
definitions.example: 24 March 1774 - Newspaper: Stamford Mercury - "Mr. Cole, Basket-maker...has lost near 300 boults of rods" https//www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0000254/17740324/001/0001

definitions.definition: A sudden spring or start; a sudden leap aside.
definitions.example: The horse made a bolt.

definitions.definition: A sudden flight, as to escape creditors.
A refusal to support a nomination made by the party with which one has been connected; a breaking away from one's party.
An iron to fasten the legs of a prisoner; a shackle; a fetter.
A burst of speed or efficiency.
To connect or assemble pieces using a bolt.
definitions.example: Bolt the vice to the bench.

definitions.definition: To secure a door by locking or barring it.
definitions.example: Bolt the door.

definitions.definition: To flee, to depart, to accelerate suddenly.
definitions.example: Seeing the snake, the horse bolted.

definitions.definition: To cause to start or spring forth; to dislodge (an animal being hunted).
definitions.example: to bolt a rabbit

definitions.definition: To strike or fall suddenly like a bolt.
To escape.
Of a plant, to grow quickly; to go to seed.
definitions.example: Lettuce and spinach will bolt as the weather warms up.

definitions.definition: To swallow food without chewing it.
To drink one's drink very quickly; to down a drink.
definitions.example: Come on, everyone, bolt your drinks; I want to go to the next pub!

definitions.definition: To refuse to support a nomination made by a party or caucus with which one has been connected; to break away from a party.
To utter precipitately; to blurt or throw out.
A sieve, especially a long fine sieve used in milling for bolting flour and meal; a bolter.
To sift, especially through a cloth.
To sift the bran and germ from wheat flour.
definitions.example: Graham flour is unbolted flour.

definitions.definition: To separate, assort, refine, or purify by other means.
To discuss or argue privately, and for practice, as cases at law.
A writing board finished with a hard white material, which can be written upon using special non-permanent markers and subsequently wiped clean.
(by extension) A collaborative tool allowing several users to write and draw on the same shared display.
A clever scheme or artful plot, usually crafted for evil purposes.
The act of machinating or plotting.
One who wades.
(chiefly in the plural) A waterproof boot that comes up to the hip, used by fishermen, etc.
A long-legged bird associated with wetland or coastal environments.
One who shoots an arrow from a bow or a bolt from a crossbow.
The bishop in chess.
An organized procession consisting of a series of consecutive displays, performances, exhibits, etc. displayed by moving down a street past a crowd of spectators.

definitions.definition: A procession of people moving down a street, organized to protest something.
Any succession, series, or display of items.

definitions.definition: A line of goslings led by one parent and often trailed by the other.
Pompous show; formal display or exhibition; outward show (as opposed to substance).
An assembling of troops for inspection or to receive orders.
Posture of defense; guard.
The ground where a military display is held, or where troops are drilled.
A public walk; a promenade; now used in street names.
definitions.example: He was parked on Chester Parade.

definitions.definition: (collective) (uncommon) A term of venery denoting a herd of elephants on the move.
To march in or as if in a procession.
definitions.example: They paraded around the field, simply to show their discipline.

definitions.definition: To cause (someone) to march in or as if in a procession; to display or show (something) during a procession.
definitions.example: They paraded dozens of fashions past the crowd.

definitions.definition: To exhibit in a showy or ostentatious manner.
To march past.
definitions.example: After the field show, it is customary to parade the stands before exiting the field.

definitions.definition: To march through or along; (of a vehicle) to move slowly through or along.
To assemble to receive orders.
To assemble (soldiers, sailors) for inspection, to receive orders, etc.
(of geese and other waterfowl) To march in a line led by one parent and often trailed by the other.
That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
The innate, mental image of an object; the link between instinct and reality.
A mineral substance made up of small crystals of silica and alumina, that is ductile when moist; the material of pre-fired ceramics.
An earth material with ductile qualities.
A tennis court surface made of crushed stone, brick, shale, or other unbound mineral aggregate.

definitions.definition: The material of the human body.
A particle less than 3.9 microns in diameter, following the Wentworth scale.
A clay pipe for smoking tobacco.
A clay pigeon.

definitions.definition: Land or territory of a country or other political region, especially when subject to territorial claims
definitions.example: Danzig is rightfully German clay.

definitions.definition: To add clay to, to spread clay onto.
(of sugar) To purify using clay.

definitions.definition: A gatekeeper; a person who guards a gate.
A chemical, such as sodium hypochlorite or hydrogen peroxide, or a preparation of such a chemical, used for disinfecting or whitening.
A variety of bleach.
To treat with bleach, especially so as to whiten (fabric, paper, etc.) or lighten (hair).
To be whitened or lightened (by the sun, for example).
(of corals) to lose color due to stress-induced expulsion of symbiotic unicellular algae.
definitions.example: Once coral bleaching begins, corals tend to continue to bleach even if the stressor is removed.

definitions.definition: To make meaningless; to divest of meaning; to make empty.
definitions.example: semantically bleached words that have become illocutionary particles

definitions.definition: An act of bleaching; exposure to the sun.
A disease of the skin.
A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.
An electrical conductor or interface serving as a common connection for two or more circuits or components.
(medical industry) An ambulance.
A main, direct public road, especially a multi-lane, high speed thoroughfare connecting major population centers.
Any public road for vehicular traffic.
A person who edits or makes changes to documents.
A copy editor.
A person who edited a specific document.

definitions.definition: A person at a newspaper, publisher or similar institution who edits stories and/or decides which ones to publish.
definitions.example: John is the city editor at the Daily Times.

definitions.definition: A machine used for editing (cutting and splicing) movie film
(computer software) A program for creating and making changes to files, especially text files.

definitions.definition: Someone who manipulates video footage and assembles it into the correct order etc for broadcast; a picture editor.
Divination about human affairs or natural phenomena from the relative positions of celestial bodies.
The name of the Latin-script letter V.
Something with the shape of the letter V.
The arc of the field, forward of the batsman, from cover to midwicket, in which drives are played
A polyamorous relationship between three people, in which one person has two partners who are not themselves romantically or sexually involved.
Any great, strong, powerful emotion, especially romantic love or extreme hate.

definitions.definition: Fervor, determination.
An object of passionate or romantic love or strong romantic interest.

definitions.definition: Sexual intercourse, especially when very emotional.

definitions.definition: (usually capitalized) The suffering of Jesus leading up to and during his crucifixion.
A display, musical composition, or play meant to commemorate the suffering of Jesus.
Suffering or enduring of imposed or inflicted pain; any suffering or distress.

definitions.definition: The state of being acted upon; subjection to an external agent or influence; a passive condition
The capacity of being affected by external agents; susceptibility of impressions from external agents.
An innate attribute, property, or quality of a thing.

definitions.definition: Disorder of the mind; madness.
To suffer pain or sorrow; to experience a passion; to be extremely agitated.
To give a passionate character to.
The desire for wealth personified as an evil spirit or a malign influence.
Often mammon: wealth, material avarice, profit.
A strip of material used for strengthening or coupling.
A long strip of material, color, etc, that is different from the surrounding area.
A strip of decoration.
That which serves as the means of union or connection between persons; a tie.
A linen collar or ruff worn in the 16th and 17th centuries.
(in the plural) Two strips of linen hanging from the neck in front as part of a clerical, legal, or academic dress.
A part of the electromagnetic spectrum.
A group of energy levels in a solid state material.

definitions.definition: A bond.
Pledge; security.
A ring, such as a wedding ring (wedding band), or a ring put on a bird's leg to identify it.
Any distinguishing line formed by chromatography, electrophoresis etc
Short for band cell.
(hiphop, often in the plural) A wad of money totaling $1K, held together by a band; (by extension) money
To fasten with a band.
To fasten an identifying band around the leg of (a bird).
A group of musicians who perform together as an ensemble, usually for a professional recording artist.
A type of orchestra originally playing janissary music.
A marching band.
A group of people loosely united for a common purpose (a band of thieves).
A small group of people living in a simple society, contrasted with tribes, chiefdoms, and states.
A group of aboriginals that has official recognition as an organized unit by the federal government of Canada.
To group together for a common purpose; to confederate.
To group (students) together by perceived ability; to stream.
A solid or hollow sphere, or roughly spherical mass.

definitions.definition: A round or ellipsoidal object.
(mildly, usually in the plural) A testicle.
A leather-covered cushion, fastened to a handle called a ballstock; formerly used by printers for inking the form, then superseded by the roller.
A large pill, a form in which medicine was given to horses; a bolus.
To form or wind into a ball.

To have sexual intercourse with.
To gather balls which cling to the feet, as of damp snow or clay; to gather into balls.

definitions.definition: (usually in present participle) To be hip or cool.
To play basketball.
To punish by affixing a ball and chain

An appeal by the crowd for holding the ball against a tackled player. This is heard almost any time an opposition player is tackled, without regard to whether the rules about "prior opportunity" to dispose of the ball are fulfilled.
An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.

definitions.definition: That part of a hall of legislation not appropriated to the official use of the assembly.

definitions.definition: A virtual area where players can chat and find opponents for a game.
An apartment or passageway in the fore part of an old-fashioned cabin under the quarter-deck.
A confined place for cattle, formed by hedges, trees, or other fencing, near the farmyard.
A margin along either side of the playing field in the sport of kabaddi.
To attempt to influence (a public official or decision-maker) in favor of a specific opinion or cause.

definitions.definition: (of a voice) Hoarse and rough-sounding.
Burly, stout.
Abounding with husks; consisting of husks.
(with the, invariable plural only) People who are old; old beings; the older generation, taken as a group.

definitions.definition: (in combination) One of a specified age.

definitions.definition: A person older than oneself, especially an adult in relation to a teenager.
(most often plural) One's parents.

definitions.definition: Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.

definitions.definition: Having been used and thus no longer new or unused.
definitions.example: I find that an old toothbrush is good to clean the keyboard with.

definitions.definition: Having existed or lived for the specified time.
definitions.example: How old are they? She’s five years old and he's seven. We also have a young teen and a two-year-old child.

definitions.definition: (heading) Of an earlier time.
Tiresome after prolonged repetition.
definitions.example: Your constant pestering is getting old.

definitions.definition: Said of subdued colors, particularly reds, pinks and oranges, as if they had faded over time.
A grammatical intensifier, often used in describing something positive. (Mostly in idioms like good old, big old and little old, any old and some old.)
definitions.example: We're having a good old time. My next car will be a big old SUV.  My wife makes the best little old apple pie in Texas.

definitions.definition: Excessive, abundant.
Extremely unpleasant or offensive; very annoying, odious or contemptible.
definitions.example: He was an especially obnoxious and detestable specimen of a man.

definitions.definition: Exposing to harm or injury.
Liquid or moisture.
Rainy weather.

definitions.definition: Rainy season. (often capitalized)
(UK politics) A moderate Conservative; especially, one who opposed the hard-line policies of British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher in the 1980s.
An alcoholic drink.
One who supports the consumption of alcohol and thus opposes Prohibition.
(in the plural) A tyre for use in wet weather.
To cover or impregnate with liquid.
To accidentally urinate in or on.

definitions.definition: To make or become wet.
(soldering) To form an intermetallic bond between a solder and a metal substrate.
To celebrate by drinking alcohol.
To kill or seriously injure.

definitions.definition: Made up of liquid or moisture, usually (but not always) water.

definitions.definition: Of an object, etc.: covered or impregnated with liquid, usually (but not always) water.

definitions.definition: Of a burrito, sandwich, or other food: covered in a sauce.
Of calligraphy and fountain pens: depositing a large amount of ink from the nib or the feed.
definitions.example: This pen’s a wet writer, so it’ll feather on this cheap paper.

definitions.definition: Of a sound recording: having had audio effects applied.
Of weather or a time period: rainy.

definitions.definition: Of a person: inexperienced in a profession or task; having the characteristics of a rookie.

definitions.definition: (of women) Sexually aroused and thus having the vulva moistened with vaginal secretions.

definitions.definition: Ineffectual, feeble, showing no strength of character.
definitions.example: Don’t be so wet.

definitions.definition: (retronym) Permitting alcoholic beverages.
Refreshed with liquor; drunk.
Of a scientist or laboratory: working with biological or chemical matter.
Employing, or done by means of, water or some other liquid.

definitions.definition: Involving assassination or "wet work".

definitions.definition: The set of letters used when writing in a language.

definitions.definition: A writing system in which letters represent phonemes. (Contrast e.g. logography, a writing system in which each character represents a word, and syllabary, in which each character represents a syllable.)
A typically finite set of distinguishable symbols.

definitions.definition: An individual letter of an alphabet; an alphabetic character.
The simplest rudiments; elements.
To designate by the letters of the alphabet; to arrange alphabetically.
An expert in virtù or art objects and antiquities; a connoisseur.
Someone with special skill or knowledge; an expert.
Specifically, a musician (or other performer) with masterly ability, technique, or personal style.
Exhibiting the ability of a virtuoso.
Of or pertaining to powder.
A cocktail consisting of gin, Campari and vermouth.
A designated player that attempts to prevent the opposing team from scoring by protecting a goal.
A person who cares for one or more babies or children for a short period of time in place of their legal guardians.

definitions.definition: A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.

definitions.definition: The quantity which constitutes a full barrel: the volume or weight this represents varies by local law and custom.
A solid drum, or a hollow cylinder or case
definitions.example: the barrel of a windlass;  the barrel of a watch, within which the spring is coiled.

definitions.definition: A metallic tube, as of a gun, from which a projectile is discharged.
A tube.
The hollow basal part of a feather.
The part of a clarinet which connects the mouthpiece and upper joint, and looks rather like a barrel (1).
A wave that breaks with a hollow compartment.
A waste receptacle.

definitions.definition: The ribs and belly of a horse or pony.
A jar.
Any of the dark-staining regions in the somatosensory cortex of rodents, etc., where somatosensory inputs from the contralateral side of the body come in from the thalamus.
A statistic derived from launch angle and exit velocity of a ball hit in play.
To put or to pack in a barrel or barrels.
To move quickly or in an uncontrolled manner.
definitions.example: He came barrelling around the corner and I almost hit him.

definitions.definition: A dusky shark.
A dusky dolphin.
A dusky grouse.
Dimly lit, as at dusk (evening).

definitions.definition: Having a shade of color that is rather dark.
definitions.example: The dusky rose was of a muted color, not clashing with any of the other colors.

definitions.definition: Dark-skinned.
Ashen; having a greyish skin coloration.

definitions.definition: Any mapped reference point on a route that can be easily identified.
One's structure or make-up: form, shape, bodily proportions.
An important or main item.

Any of the physical constituents of the face (eyes, nose, etc.).
A beneficial capability of a piece of software.
The cast or structure of anything, or of any part of a thing, as of a landscape, a picture, a treaty, or an essay; any marked peculiarity or characteristic.

definitions.definition: Something discerned from physical evidence that helps define, identify, characterize, and interpret an archeological site.
Characteristic forms or shapes of parts. For example, a hole, boss, slot, cut, chamfer, or fillet.
(machine learning) An individual measurable property or characteristic of a phenomenon being observed.
The act of being featured in a piece of music.
The elements into which linguistic units can be broken down.
To ascribe the greatest importance to something within a certain context.
To star, to contain.
To appear, to make an appearance.
To have features resembling.
An act of retiring; withdrawal.
The state of being retired; seclusion.
A place of seclusion or privacy; a retreat.
The state of having permanently left one's employment, now especially at reaching pensionable age; the portion of one's life after retiring from one's career.
The act of leaving one's career or employment permanently.

A male escort at a wedding.
A doorkeeper in a courtroom.
An assistant to a head teacher or schoolteacher; an assistant teacher.
Any schoolteacher.
To guide people to their seats.
To accompany or escort (someone).
To precede; to act as a forerunner or herald.
To lead or guide somewhere
The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event

definitions.definition: A lightweight container, generally round, open at the top, and tapering toward the bottom.

definitions.definition: A wire or plastic container similar in shape to a basket, used for carrying articles for purchase in a shop.
In an online shop, a notional place to store items before ordering them.
A circular hoop, from which a net is suspended, which is the goal through which the players try to throw the ball.

The bulge of the male genitals seen through clothing.
In a stage-coach, two outside seats facing each other.
A protection for the hand on a sword or a singlestick; a guard of a bladed weapon.

Bastard.

definitions.definition: To place in a basket or baskets.
To cross-collateralize the royalty advances for multiple works so that the creator is not paid until all of those works have achieved a certain level of success.
Located along a bay
Without planning or thinking ahead.

definitions.definition: Careless; without sufficient thought or consideration.

definitions.definition: Curt, abrupt, unfriendly.

definitions.definition: Right away, immediately, without thinking about it.

definitions.definition: In an offhand manner.
Given to wilful, perverse deviation from the expected norm; tending to stray
Obstinate, contrary and unpredictable
Not on target
(maritime) The ship occupied by the fleet's commander (usually an admiral); it denotes this by flying his flag.
(maritime) The ship regarded as most important out of a group, e.g. a nation's navy or company's fleet.
(by extension) The most important one out of a related group.

definitions.definition: To act as a flagship for
To cease from action, motion, work, or performance of any kind; stop; desist; be without motion.
To come to a pause or an end; end.
To be free from that which harasses or disturbs; be quiet or still; be undisturbed.
To be or to put into a state of rest.

definitions.definition: To stay, remain, be situated.
definitions.example: The blame seems to rest with your father.

definitions.definition: To lean, lie, or lay.

definitions.definition: To complete one's active advocacy in a trial or other proceeding, and thus to wait for the outcome (however, one is still generally available to answer questions, etc.)

definitions.definition: To sleep; slumber.
To lie dormant.
To sleep the final sleep; sleep in death; die; be dead.
To rely or depend on.

To remain.
To arrest.

Remaining; stationary.

The ball used in the above sport.
A mammal, Capra aegagrus hircus, and similar species of the genus Capra.
A lecherous man.
A scapegoat.
A Pontiac GTO car.

A fool, loser, or object of ridicule.
To allow goats to feed on.
To scapegoat.
The state of being latent.
A delay, a period between the initiation of something and the occurrence.

A stage in Sigmund Freud's psychoanalytic theory of the psychosexual development of children where children become asexual until their sexual desires come back at puberty.
One who deals in goods, especially automobiles; a middleman.

definitions.definition: A drug dealer, one who peddles illicit drugs.
A particular type of stock broker or trader.
The person who deals the cards in a card game.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
Offbeat, unconventional or eccentric.
Not quite right; of questionable quality; not appropriate to the context.
Cool; great; excellent.
Having or relating to the smell of funk.
Relating to or reminiscent of various genres of African American music, especially funk.
Relating to, or characterized by, great fear, or funking.
To cause to shine briefly or intermittently.

definitions.definition: To blink; to shine or illuminate intermittently.
definitions.example: The light flashed on and off.

definitions.definition: To be visible briefly.

definitions.definition: To make visible briefly.
definitions.example: A number will be flashed on the screen.

definitions.definition: To show or expose an "inappropriate" part of the body to someone for humorous reasons or as an act of contempt.
To break forth like a sudden flood of light; to show a momentary brilliance.
To flaunt; to display in a showy manner.

definitions.definition: To communicate quickly.

definitions.definition: To move, or cause to move, suddenly.

definitions.definition: To telephone a person, only allowing the phone to ring once, in order to request a call back.

definitions.definition: (of liquid) To evaporate suddenly. (See flash evaporation.)
To climb (a route) successfully on the first attempt.
To write to the memory of (an updatable component such as a BIOS chip or games cartridge).
(glassmaking) To cover with a thin layer, as objects of glass with glass of a different colour.
(glassmaking) To expand (blown glass) into a disc.

To burst out into violence.
To perform a flash.
To release the pressure from a pressurized vessel.
To trick up in a showy manner.
To strike and throw up large bodies of water from the surface; to splash.
A person who needlessly destroys, defaces, or damages other people's property.
To reestablish, or bring back into existence.

To give in place of, or as restitution for.

To make good; to make amends for.

A paparazzo.
Paparazzi taken as a group.

To treat like a nanny's charges; to coddle.

The vulva.

Class; kind.
(grammar) A division of nouns and pronouns (and sometimes of other parts of speech) into masculine or feminine, and sometimes other categories like neuter or common, and animate or inanimate.
(now sometimes proscribed) Sex (a category such as "male" or "female" into which sexually-reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species).
definitions.example: The effect of the medication is dependent upon age, gender, and other factors.

definitions.definition: Identification as a man, a woman or something else, and association with a (social) role or set of behavioral and cultural traits, clothing, etc; a category to which a person belongs on this basis. (Compare gender role, gender identity.)

(hardware) The quality which distinguishes connectors, which may be male (fitting into another connector) and female (having another connector fit into it), or genderless/androgynous (capable of fitting together with another connector of the same type).
To assign a gender to (a person); to perceive as having a gender; to address using terms (pronouns, nouns, adjectives...) that express a certain gender.
To perceive (a thing) as having characteristics associated with a certain gender, or as having been authored by someone of a certain gender.

definitions.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.

definitions.definition: Sound made through vibration of the vocal cords; sonant, or intonated, utterance; tone; — distinguished from mere breath sound as heard in whispering and voiceless consonants.
The tone or sound emitted by an object
The faculty or power of utterance
definitions.example: to cultivate the voice

definitions.definition: That which is communicated; message; meaning.
An expressed opinion, choice, will, desire, or wish; the right or ability to make such expression or to have it considered
Command; precept.
One who speaks; a speaker.
A particular style or way of writing that expresses a certain tone or feeling.

(grammar) A particular way of inflecting or conjugating verbs, or a particular form of a verb, by means of which is indicated the relation of the subject of the verb to the action which the verb expresses.
definitions.example: The verbal system of Latin has two voices, active and passive.

definitions.definition: In harmony, an independent vocal or instrumental part in a piece of composition.
definitions.example: The theme of this piece constantly migrates between the three voice parts.

definitions.definition: (IRC) A flag associated with a user on a channel, determining whether or not they can send messages to the channel.
To stupefy by doping with chemical substances such as alcohol.
To excite to enthusiasm or madness.
Stupefied by alcohol, drunk.
Stupefied by any chemical substance.
To furnish with decorations.
definitions.example: We decorated the Christmas tree with tinsel and baubles.

definitions.definition: To improve the appearance of an interior of, as a house, room, or office.
definitions.example: There's some paint left over from when we decorated the guest bedroom.

definitions.definition: To decorate an interior space, as a house, room, or office.
definitions.example: People tend to decorate for the holidays or special events.

definitions.definition: To honor by providing a medal, ribbon, or other adornment.
definitions.example: He was a decorated soldier who served in three wars.

definitions.definition: To extend a method, etc. by attaching some further code item.
definitions.example: It makes sure that the field name argument is not empty, and that the field specified there is an actual existing field in the class which declares the method decorated with this attribute.

definitions.definition: A member of an evangelical church
An advocate of evangelicalism
Pertaining to the doctrines or teachings of the Christian gospel or Christianity in general.
Pertaining to the gospel(s) of the Christian New Testament.
Protestant; specifically Lutheran and Calvinist churches in continental Europe as well as their offshoots in North America.
Pertaining to a movement in Protestant Christianity that stresses personal conversion and the authority of the Bible (evangelicalism).
Pertaining to Islamic groups that are dedicated to dawah and preaching the Quran and sunnah.
Zealously enthusiastic.
A situation, in a soap opera or similar serial fiction, in which a new storyline explains or changes a previous event or attaches a new significance to it.
To employ such a device.
The act of realizing.
The result of an artistic effort.
definitions.example: The painter's realization of her concept was breathtaking.

definitions.definition: The plant Rubus idaeus.
Any of many other (but not all) species in the genus Rubus.
The juicy aggregate fruit of these plants.
A red colour, the colour of a ripe raspberry.
To gather or forage for raspberries.
Containing or having the flavor/flavour of raspberries.
Of a dark pinkish red.
A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering people from the weather.
definitions.example: We were camping in a three-man tent.

definitions.definition: The representation of a tent used as a bearing.
A portable pulpit set up outside to accommodate worshippers who cannot fit into a church.
A trouser tent; a piece of fabric, etc. protruding outward like a tent.
To go camping.
definitions.example: We’ll be tented at the campground this weekend.

definitions.definition: To prop up aluminum foil in an inverted "V" (reminiscent of a pop-up tent) over food to reduce splatter, before putting it in the oven.
To form into a tent-like shape.
definitions.example: The sheet tented over his midsection.

definitions.definition: To make such a sound.
To travel very fast, as if making the sounds of brakes being released
The act of producing a screech.
definitions.example: hysterical screechings

definitions.definition: The use of state or group power to control freedom of expression or press, such as passing laws to prevent media from being published or propagated.
The role of the censor (magistrate) in Ancient Rome.
The sound made by a hen, especially when brooding, or calling her chicks.
Any sound similar to this.
A kind of tongue click used to urge on a horse.
To make such a sound.
To cause (the tongue) to make a clicking sound.
definitions.example: My mother clucked her tongue in disapproval.

definitions.definition: To call together, or call to follow, as a hen does her chickens.
To suffer withdrawal from heroin.
A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.
Anything that binds two things or people together.
Birdlime.
To join or attach something using glue.
definitions.example: I need to glue the chair-leg back into place.

definitions.definition: To cause something to adhere closely to; to follow attentively.
definitions.example: His eyes were glued to the screen.

definitions.definition: Phony, as if a hoax; noticeably contrived; of obviously flimsy credibility or quality
definitions.example: I thought the windshield wiper blades were a little hokey when I saw their cheap packaging.

definitions.definition: Corny; overly or unbelievably sentimental
definitions.example: Terry hated going to the cinema with Pat, she always chose hokey romantic comedies that made him want to gag.

definitions.definition: The state of being insusceptible to something; notably:
A resistance to a specific thing.
definitions.example: Superbugs are bacteria that develop an immunity to antibiotics.

synonyms: get out of jail free card

definitions.definition: Ready to accept instruction or direction; obedient; subservient.
Yielding to control or supervision, direction, or management.
To request forcefully.
definitions.example: I demand to see the manager.

definitions.definition: To claim a right to something.
definitions.example: The bank is demanding the mortgage payment.

definitions.definition: To ask forcefully for information.
definitions.example: I demand an immediate explanation.

definitions.definition: To require of someone.
definitions.example: This job demands a lot of patience.

definitions.definition: To issue a summons to court.
Requiring much endurance, strength, or patience.
